name: Primordial Soil
description: The first starter preset — a trophic-realistic microbial load on the 32x32 world. A producer-heavy biomass pyramid (autotroph base >> decomposers > predator), the way a real soil/biofilm community is structured. Designed to be ALIVE + LEGIBLE + show emergent trophic dynamics (producer growth, decomposition, predation, spore counterplay) without being chaotic. Containment starts Sealed so the player sees the designed community's closed dynamics first; raise Containment to let airborne contaminants trickle in (the open-system reality).

roster.label: Abstract plant (autotroph)
roster.note: the energy base — producers dominate the biomass pyramid; grows on solar influx

roster.label: E. coli K-12 (decomposer)
roster.note: primary consumer of detritus from dead producers

roster.label: Bacillus (spore-former)
roster.note: a second decomposer — adds spore/dormancy dynamics + cull counterplay (a cull leaves a regerminating spore bank)

roster.label: Bdellovibrio (predator)
roster.note: apex predator of E. coli — sparse (predators are rare in a healthy pyramid)

containment.note: Sealed for a clean first impression of the closed community; the consortium is pre-armed so raising Containment to Lab/Open immediately starts deterministic airborne immigration.

_design_notes: Counts are a producer-heavy pyramid ~ 16:5:3:1 (800:250:150:50), ~1250 orgs on 1024 cells (~1.2/cell). Total stays light so the tick is fast + the map legible. These counts are a STARTING POINT — the autonomous emergent-run discovery harness (docs/llm/proposals/emergent-discovery-harness-draft.md) is the long-term way to tune presets + find dramatic runs. Symbionts (carsonella/syn3) are intentionally excluded from the airborne consortium (they can't free-live / airborne-arrive) — a host-coupled symbiont preset is a separate Mode-B starter.
